In Loving Memory

Steve

April 3, 1968 — November 19, 2022

Husband. Father of five. Coach, fisherman, guitar player — and the best man most of us ever knew.

Chapter One

He was just a really good man.

Steve grew up in Blanchard, Oklahoma and never really left — because everything he loved was right there. He married Julie on May 8, 1994, and spent 28 years building a life that most people only dream about.

Five kids — Stephanie, Holly, Stephen, Colten, and Jack. One grandson, Boston. A house that was never quiet and always full of love, sports on TV, and somebody needing something from Dad.

He coached baseball. He showed up to every game whether he was officially coaching or not. He taught himself guitar and played front yard concerts just because it made him happy. Whenever he got the chance he was outside — rod in hand at the pond, hunting before sunrise, breathing fresh Oklahoma air.

"He was always the sideline coach — even when he wasn't officially one. That was just who he was."

From the people who loved him

Steve passed away on November 19, 2022. He was 54 years old — way too young, way too soon. This site is for him. Not a museum. Just a place to tell his story.

Read his obituary → Sign the guestbook →
Chapter Two

They remember him.

Real words from real people who knew Steve Kiker.

“

He was a fantastic athlete and a better person. Stevie was a great leader and had just the right amount of rebel in him. He was a blast to coach. If I was going to a fist fight in an alley, Steve Kiker would be my first choice to go with me. The word quit was not in him.

George Machado His High School Football Coach — Waukesha, WI

"I met Kiker in the fifth grade and we had a lot of good times together. He was a star athlete and always someone to look up to. I will miss you my friend."

Les WillardFriend — Oklahoma City, OK

"This man had such an influence and impact on my life when I was a kid. I never forgot his work ethic and sense of humor."

Larry EdgemonClassmate — McKinney, TX

"In loving memory of a wonderful person. We will love you and miss you always. A True Best Friend."

Terry MayFriend — Blanchard, OK

"We will miss seeing Steve outside and hearing him play his guitar. Julie, please let us know if there is anything we can do to help."

Darrell & CharlaNeighbors — Blanchard, OK

"Sorry I wasn't at the funeral. One of my favorite cousins. I always enjoyed seeing him play baseball and the July fourth get togethers were great!"

Sheila TioutiFamily — Tulsa, OK

"I have great memories of Steve. Condolences to Steve's family and friends."

Kelly ChambersClassmate — Norman, OK
The kind of man he was

You would have liked him.

👑
Never Missed a Game

Didn't matter whose game it was. If a kid was playing, Steve was there.

🗣
Never Met a Stranger

Could make anyone laugh in under thirty seconds. It was a gift.

🛠
Fixed Everything

If something broke, Steve showed up. No questions asked.

❤
28 Years Strong

Married Julie on May 8, 1994. Loved her every single day of it.

🏠
Blanchard Was Home

Born in Holdenville. Moved to Blanchard in 5th grade. Never needed to leave.

STEVE
Steve's Coin

Honestly? We just love him.

There's no big plan here. We just missed him and didn't want to sit around doing nothing about it. So we made this — a coin with his name on it. A place to keep him out in the world a little longer.

No big plan. Just love.

❤️ One last thing

Please go get your heart checked.

Steve was 54. He was active. He was happy. He had absolutely no idea anything was wrong.

Heart disease doesn't give warnings. It doesn't care how good of a man you are or how many people still need you around.

Know your numbers. Get checked. Tell the people you love that you love them — today, not later.

That's what Steve would have wanted you to take from all of this.

Steve Kiker

April 3, 1968  —  November 19, 2022

Blanchard, Oklahoma

Always the sideline coach
